jokes.text: So, is there a lot of improv on the show? Yes, there's a script, but I make up most of my own lines, put my own spin on the words, make it better.
jokes.setup: Standard talk show question about improvisation on Curb
jokes.punchline: Maria Sofia's absurdly arrogant claim that she makes the show better
jokes.explanation: Perfect character introduction - establishes Maria Sofia's delusional confidence that will drive conflicts throughout the episode. Well-structured irony.

jokes.text: Could you just stop with that... Stop with that commercial? I don't wanna hear that. Don't sing that in the house. I wanna stop. I can't stop!
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about Cheryl singing a commercial jingle
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's admission she literally can't control herself
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry domestic irritation, but the involuntary nature adds a nice twist. Solid setup for his general annoyance.

jokes.text: Larry complaining about someone singing the J.G. Wentworth commercial jingle repeatedly
jokes.setup: Catchy commercial jingle being sung
jokes.punchline: Larry's frustrated 'Could you just stop with that... Don't sing that in the house'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry annoyance at mundane social irritation - earworms and commercial jingles

jokes.text: Oh, Mr. 'I'm never gonna stop drinking almond milk.' Because what did I tell you about almond milk? You know, the water that they use in this state for these almonds and the methophexohexadine, they're killing the bees!
jokes.setup: Larry has apparently switched from almond to oat milk
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's made-up chemical name and overwrought environmental concern
jokes.explanation: The invented chemical name 'methophexohexadine' is genuinely funny made-up word, and the bee-killing concern is perfectly Cheryl-esque environmental anxiety.

jokes.text: Cheryl's pseudo-scientific explanation about almond milk: 'the methophexohexadine, they're killing the bees!'
jokes.setup: Explaining why almond milk is bad
jokes.punchline: Made-up chemical name 'methophexohexadine'
jokes.explanation: Funny made-up scientific-sounding chemical name, good absurdist humor

jokes.text: I'm sorry you're unhappy, Larry, but as her sponsor, I'm telling you, she simply cannot handle a breakup. How long are we talking? Well, they say no changes for the first 90 days in the program, but with Irma, I mean, minimum six months.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to break up with someone named Irma
jokes.punchline: Cheryl forbidding the breakup for six months due to addiction recovery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant premise - Larry trapped in a relationship by 12-step program rules. The escalation from 90 days to 6 months is perfect.

jokes.text: Larry's circular conversation about not going to the golf club while being asked to pick up hammer toe medicine 'if' he goes
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking him to pick something up 'if' he goes to golf club
jokes.punchline: Larry's frustrated insistence that he's not going, but she keeps saying 'if'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry frustration with illogical social convention, well-structured circular argument

jokes.text: Siri, directions to Wolfsglen. Directions to Great Wolf Lodge. No, Siri, Wolfsglen Restaurant.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to get directions from Siri
jokes.punchline: Siri completely misunderstanding despite clear pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Relatable tech frustration but not particularly original. Sets up the escalation nicely though.

jokes.text: No, you stupid fucking idiot! It's Wolfsglen Restaurant in Westwood! One option I see, Jay Wolf Clothing. No! Ah, you fuck! Wolfsglen Restaurant!
jokes.setup: Larry's complete meltdown at Siri's continued failures
jokes.punchline: Calling Siri increasingly vulgar names while it keeps mishearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry escalation - his rage at inanimate objects is a character hallmark. The progression of insults works well.

jokes.text: Somebody is paying you to just show up at a birthday party in Atlanta. Yeah. I mean, so stupid. Yeah, rich guy. They're paying him a ton of money.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining Larry's paid appearance gig
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive 'so stupid' about easy money
jokes.explanation: Larry's contempt for easy money while accepting it anyway is perfectly in character. Sets up the Atlanta plot.

jokes.text: Larry's specific contract demands: no speeches, no stand-up, no toasts, just mingling and handshaking
jokes.setup: Discussing what Larry will do at the party
jokes.punchline: His very specific limitations on social interaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's social anxiety and need for control

jokes.text: Jeff dismissing Larry's appeal with 'She's the draw' when Maria Sofia is mentioned
jokes.setup: Larry confused about why he's being paid for appearance
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact deflation that Maria Sofia is the real attraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff delivery - brutal honesty about Larry's diminished status

jokes.text: He's a wealthy businessman from Africa who loves Young Larry. Oh, well, now it makes sense. She's the draw.
jokes.setup: Revealing Maria Sofia will also be at the party
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-deprecating realization that she's the real attraction
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware moment for Larry. The quick deflation of his ego is economically delivered.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna do any speeches. I'm not gonna do any stand-up. I'm not gonna do any toasts. I'll mingle, I'll shake hands, and I'll be cordial. Do you even know how to be cordial? I think so. Do I? I would guess you do. I don't think I can, but I'll try.
jokes.setup: Larry listing what he won't do at the party
jokes.punchline: His genuine uncertainty about his ability to be cordial
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-awareness from Larry about his social limitations. The word 'cordial' becomes crucial to the episode's conflict.

jokes.text: The server Benny's mother dying creating an impossible complaint situation
jokes.setup: Food taking forever to arrive
jokes.punchline: Can't complain because the server's mother just died
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry scenario - trapped by social convention in an impossible situation

jokes.text: Few days ago and he comes into work? What's he coming in for? If he's all upset, then he should be home in mourning.
jokes.setup: Learning about Benny's mother's death
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical but insensitive reaction about working while grieving
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry logic - technically correct but socially tone-deaf. His inability to just accept the situation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And you can't complain, obviously. No, you can't go to the manager. It's a no-win situation. Yeah, and you know what else? Now he gets a condolence tip.
jokes.setup: Discussing being trapped by the server's tragedy
jokes.punchline: Larry realizing they now owe a 'condolence tip'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark observation - the idea of a 'condolence tip' as social obligation is both horrible and hilarious. Perfect Larry social analysis.

jokes.text: This is a... This is new. I don't know what you're talking about. Uh, you decided to just dye your hair? What are you doing that for? You sure Lady Macbeth didn't have anything to do with that?
jokes.setup: Larry noticing Jeff dyed his gray hair brown
jokes.punchline: Calling Jeff's wife Lady Macbeth, implying she made him do it
jokes.explanation: Clever Shakespeare reference that paints Susie as manipulative. The Lady Macbeth comparison is both literary and cutting.

jokes.text: Larry confronting Jeff about dyeing his hair and suggesting Lady Macbeth (Susie) made him do it
jokes.setup: Larry noticing Jeff's newly dyed hair
jokes.punchline: The Lady Macbeth reference for Susie's influence
jokes.explanation: Good literary reference for Susie's controlling nature, typical Larry directness about appearance changes

jokes.text: You wish you had a wife that cared about you. You wish you had a wife. I almost did a... I almost did a spit take.
jokes.setup: Jeff's comeback about Larry wishing he had a caring wife
jokes.punchline: Larry nearly doing a literal spit take at the burn
jokes.explanation: Good comeback from Jeff, and Larry's self-aware spit take comment adds meta humor. Solid banter.

jokes.text: Leon's concept of 'dick dialing' people by accident from his front pocket
jokes.setup: Discussing butt dialing vs. front pocket dialing
jokes.punchline: Leon's casual use of 'dick dial' as the male equivalent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay invention by Leon, perfectly in character and highly quotable

jokes.text: This is unbelievable. And there's my soup! It's sitting there, getting cold. Hey! I ordered a tuna melt. That's fucked. But it was the sepsis that ultimately killed her.
jokes.setup: Larry frustrated about cold soup while Benny shares grief
jokes.punchline: Larry cursing about the wrong order while death details continue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of Larry's trivial food complaints with sepsis details. The overlapping dialogue creates uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: You can't do that shit to old people, man. I mean, I almost killed a uncle like that. Yeah. This motherfucker was in the tub taking a fucking bath. He's like, 'What the fuck?' That was it. Motherfucker died like this.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining you can't surprise old people
jokes.punchline: Graphic story about accidentally killing his uncle with surprise
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon - casual profanity mixed with darkly absurd family anecdote. His storytelling style is perfectly captured.

jokes.text: I know what you mean. Why do you keep asking me if I know what you mean? It wasn't that difficult. It's not science. You made a very simple statement. Yeah, I know what you mean.
jokes.setup: Leon repeatedly asking 'know what I mean?'
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical breakdown of why the phrase is unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry pet peeve - analyzing conversational tics with ruthless logic. His irritation at redundant phrases is very in character.

jokes.text: Maria Sofia's casual 'Oh, the plane won't take off without me' when called out for being late
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on her being late
jokes.punchline: Her delusional sense of importance to flight operations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maria Sofia ego moment, absurd self-importance delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Leon demonstrating by calling the dog multiple insults: 'Hey, fat fuck. You a fat ass. You fucking fat. You a short, fat ass dog.'
jokes.setup: Explaining dogs don't understand insults
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic demonstration with increasingly specific insults
jokes.explanation: Hilarious escalation of the dog insult concept, perfectly over-the-top Leon behavior

jokes.text: Leon warning the dog about airplane food: 'Don't you eat nothing else' and noting the dog understood that part
jokes.setup: His theory that dogs don't understand insults
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicted by the dog responding to food warnings
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that undercuts Leon's theory, good structure with immediate contradiction

jokes.text: And sometimes, I can hear my dick dialing a number. Know what I mean? I'm like, 'What the fuck are you doing?' You know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Building on the dick-dialing concept
jokes.punchline: Leon having conversations with his phone-dialing penis
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Leon arguing with his own penis adds another layer of absurdity. The casualness makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: This is my emotional support dog, Pechuca. Emotional support dog? What a scam. They shouldn't even let you on the plane if you're that mentally unstable.
jokes.setup: Maria Sofia introducing her emotional support dog
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt assessment that emotional support animals are scams
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social observation - calling out what many think but won't say. His directness about mental instability is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: You can't insult a dog. They don't know. Yes, she does. You need self-awareness to understand someone's shaming you. This dog is not gonna turn around and go, 'What the fuck you say?' Look, watch this. Hey, fat fuck. You a fat ass. You fucking fat. You a short, fat ass dog.
jokes.setup: Argument about whether dogs understand insults
jokes.punchline: Leon demonstrating by directly insulting the dog to prove it doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Leon's willingness to verbally abuse a dog to make a philosophical point is hilariously inappropriate and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Hotel housekeeper giving Larry an eye roll and saying 'I come back' after seeing his messy room
jokes.setup: Larry opening door for housekeeping
jokes.punchline: Housekeeper's judgmental eye roll and dismissive departure
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy moment, sets up Larry's confusion about normal hotel behavior

jokes.text: This... this is a typical room. There's clothes strewn about. Yes, I see there's underwear and socks. I come back. What? What did I... That's what people do when they're in a hotel. Socks, underwear on the floor. You don't care. You're not... you're not home.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to defend his messy hotel room
jokes.punchline: His logical argument that hotel messiness is normal behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic - completely reasonable but delivered in a way that makes him seem defensive. His hotel behavior philosophy is sound.

jokes.text: I guarantee you there'll be a tip for you in this room. Guaranteed. I come back. I feel like somehow I've disappointed you. I come back later. What have I done? I'm just staying... in a hotel. Is this your first room you've ever cleaned? This is what a hotel is.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to appease the judgmental housekeeper
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate attempts to justify normal hotel behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's bewilderment and guilt. His final question about it being her first room is a perfect Larry response - logical but insulting.

jokes.text: I had a 4.0 APA, 'Ass-Point-Average.' In high school, I was the valedictorian of tapping ass.
jokes.setup: Leon looking at his high school photo
jokes.punchline: Turning GPA into 'Ass-Point-Average' and claiming to be valedictorian of sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon wordplay - 'Ass-Point-Average' is instantly quotable and the valedictorian escalation is perfect. Classic Leon sexual braggadocio.

jokes.text: Larry commenting on Auntie Rae's food: 'That does not look good at all. It really looks inedible.'
jokes.setup: Being invited to try the hors d'oeuvres
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt assessment of the food quality
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry rudeness about food, perfectly inappropriate social behavior

jokes.text: Maria Sofia taking Larry's glasses without asking and him discovering they're stretched out
jokes.setup: Maria Sofia trying on Larry's glasses
jokes.punchline: Glasses being stretched out by her large head
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup, establishes ongoing glasses problem

jokes.text: They don't fit. Oh, my goodness. You stretched them out. No, I didn't. What'd you do? Look at the size of her head. Are you kidding? You can't just take a pair of glasses and try them on. You have a big head. Dare I say, freakish?
jokes.setup: Larry's glasses no longer fit after Auntie Rae wore them
jokes.punchline: Larry calling her head 'freakish' in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry rudeness - calling an elderly woman's head 'freakish' is perfectly inappropriate and creates maximum cringe. The escalation is natural.

jokes.text: It's excessive. It's like a jack-o'-lantern. You know what? You got a peanut head like Mr. Peanut.
jokes.setup: Continuing the head size argument
jokes.punchline: Comparing her head to a jack-o'-lantern and his to Mr. Peanut
jokes.explanation: Great visual comparisons - jack-o'-lantern and Mr. Peanut are both specific and funny. The escalating head insults work perfectly.

jokes.text: Larry comparing Maria Sofia's head to a 'jack-o'-lantern' and her comeback about his 'peanut head'
jokes.setup: Argument about head sizes
jokes.punchline: Dueling head size insults with specific comparisons
jokes.explanation: Childish insult escalation with creative visual comparisons

jokes.text: Get your fat ass off my jacket. You crazy? Pechuca is not fat. I told your pudgy ass before to stay off my shit. Do not talk to Pechuca like that.
jokes.setup: Dog sitting on Leon's jacket
jokes.punchline: Leon's aggressive confrontation with both dog and owner
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the fat dog discussion. Leon's willingness to argue with both pet and owner is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Larry's assessment of how Auntie Rae's loaner glasses look: 'They look fucking ridiculous with a peanut fucking head'
jokes.setup: Trying on the replacement glasses
jokes.punchline: Using Maria Sofia's insult against himself
jokes.explanation: Good callback usage, Larry accepting and using the insult against himself

jokes.text: How do they look? They look fucking ridiculous with a peanut fucking head.
jokes.setup: Larry trying on Auntie Rae's glasses
jokes.punchline: Auntie Rae's brutal assessment using his own insult back at him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback revenge - Auntie Rae using Larry's own cruel description against him. The economy and timing are excellent.

jokes.text: If a guest dropped glasses in the toilet, who fishes the glasses out of the toilet? Is that your department? Or is it the guest who's never put his hands in a toilet in his life? Or the housekeeper who's very familiar with it and is kind of desensitized, in a way, to the whole situation and has gloves and has the equipment and a plan?
jokes.setup: Larry posing a 'hypothetical' about glasses in toilet
jokes.punchline: His increasingly elaborate justification for why housekeeping should retrieve them
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logical argument for avoiding unpleasant task. His elaborate reasoning to justify having someone else do it is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Larry's detailed argument about why housekeepers should retrieve toilet glasses (gloves, equipment, desensitization)
jokes.setup: The hypothetical toilet glasses question
jokes.punchline: His logical but inappropriate reasoning for delegating gross tasks
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic applied to inappropriate situation, detailed reasoning makes it funnier

jokes.text: You get 'em yourself! What? Hey! They're not mine. I never said they were my glasses. That was a hypothetical. A hypothetical means it's possible and maybe not possible. It's just a question.
jokes.setup: Housekeeper telling Larry to get the glasses himself
jokes.punchline: Larry's frantic backpedaling that it was just hypothetical
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the hypothetical setup. Larry's desperate explanation of what 'hypothetical' means shows his panic at being caught.

jokes.text: You know, you're quite a farbissiner.
jokes.setup: Housekeeper being uncooperative
jokes.punchline: Larry using Yiddish word for sour person
jokes.explanation: Nice Yiddish vocabulary from Larry. The word choice is character-appropriate and the timing works as a final insult.

jokes.text: Larry calling Ligaya 'quite a farbissiner' (Yiddish for sour person)
jokes.setup: Ligaya's negative reaction to his questions
jokes.punchline: Larry using obscure Yiddish insult
jokes.explanation: Good use of Yiddish, perfectly fits Larry's character and gives variety to his insults

jokes.text: I thought you were African. Yeah, I am. I'm South African. Born and raised in Joburg. You don't tell people you're from Africa if you're from South Africa.
jokes.setup: Larry meeting the party host Michael Fouchay
jokes.punchline: Larry's geographical pedantry about Africa vs South Africa
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry geographical nitpicking. Sets up his eventual rant about Michael not being 'really' African.

jokes.text: If this motherfucker's African, I'm fucking a... What's the little boy in that fucking book with the freckles on his face, with the overalls and the fucking bare feet, and he looked wet all the time? Huckleberry Finn.
jokes.setup: Leon's disbelief that Michael is African
jokes.punchline: His elaborate, profane description of Huckleberry Finn
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon moment - his roundabout, profane way of reaching 'Huckleberry Finn' is hilariously circuitous and perfectly captures his speech pattern.

jokes.text: You know, when people tell me I'm going to love someone, I'm kind of programmed not to like them. So it's kind of a lose-lose for Brookie.
jokes.setup: Michael saying Larry will love meeting Brooke
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-aware admission that pre-selling someone dooms the relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry social observation - the psychology of being told you'll love someone. Self-aware and relatable, setting up the Brooke conflict.

jokes.text: Oh, only her really close friends call her Brookie. So I can't call her Brookie? No. No. Can I introduce you to some of my guests?
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the Brookie nickname rule
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate desire to use the forbidden name
jokes.explanation: Key setup for the episode's main conflict. Larry's immediate interest in breaking the rule is perfectly predictable and in character.

jokes.text: They put clear where they should put green. Green... They put clear in the green and the green in the clear. You understand? Yeah, sure. The green in the clear... Has this happened to you?
jokes.setup: Fan trying to relate to Larry about grocery bag organization
jokes.punchline: Her confusing explanation and Larry's polite but bewildered responses
jokes.explanation: Mild party small talk awkwardness. The confusing bag explanation creates low-level cringe but isn't particularly memorable.

jokes.text: The fan's confusing story about grocery store bag placement that trails off incomprehensibly
jokes.setup: Fan trying to relate to Larry with mundane observation
jokes.punchline: Her completely incoherent explanation about green and clear bags
jokes.explanation: Realistic portrayal of awkward fan interaction but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Have you noticed that when you take a picture, you don't look nearly as good as you do when you look in the mirror? Yeah. Because the mirror is how you see yourself. And the photo is how you're seen. Fuck, that's deep.
jokes.setup: Larry making observation about mirrors vs photos
jokes.punchline: Leon's impressed 'Fuck, that's deep' response
jokes.explanation: Decent Larry observation about self-perception. Leon's response adds the humor - treating a fairly obvious insight as profound.

jokes.text: I wonder if a Black man going to Africa is like a Jew going to Israel. If you go to Israel to fuck, you know what I'm saying? Let's plan a trip. We'll hit the Congo... And then, we'll go to Tel Aviv. I'll tap yours. You tap mine.
jokes.setup: Leon comparing Black/Africa to Jew/Israel experience
jokes.punchline: Proposing mutual hookup tourism between Congo and Tel Aviv
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon - turning cultural identity into sexual tourism opportunity. The mutual exchange proposal is perfectly crude and funny.

jokes.text: You seem to be in violation of the Brooke-Brookie rule. Please, educate me. Yeah. How am I in violation of this rule? Well, only her close friends can call her Brookie. I'm gonna get to the bottom of this.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the rule to Carl
jokes.punchline: His promise to investigate the inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - turning a simple social preference into a full investigation. His legalistic approach to nicknames is hilariously inappropriate.

jokes.text: Now, Carl, who hardly knows you at all, calls you Brookie, whereas I was given very strict instructions, by you, to call you Brooke. Carl is a great guy. No, no! Carl is not a great guy. I just spoke to Carl. I didn't find Carl to be a great guy at all.
jokes.setup: Larry confronting Brooke about the double standard
jokes.punchline: His immediate dismissal of Carl based on brief conversation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Larry's instant judgment of Carl and systematic dismantling of Brooke's defense is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: If I had a Larr-Larry rule, Carl wouldn't be calling me Larr. I can tell you that. Oh, I'm so sorry. Do you want me to call you Larr? No, because I don't have a Larr-Larry rule. I don't have a Brooke-Brookie Rule. No, you do. You do have a Brooke-Brookie rule. And it only applies to me.
jokes.setup: Larry creating hypothetical Larr-Larry rule
jokes.punchline: His accusation that the rule specifically targets him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - the Larr-Larry parallel is perfect and his persecution complex is spot-on. This is peak Larry social analysis and paranoia.

jokes.text: I'm gonna be Desmond Tutu and then I'm gonna ask you questions. You're gonna be Tutu? Yeah, because Tutu was the guy asking the questions. But in this particular case, I was a victim. You shouldn't be Tutu. I should be Tutu. We can't both be Tutu. We can't have two Tutus.
jokes.setup: Michael proposing Truth and Reconciliation roleplay
jokes.punchline: Argument over who gets to be Desmond Tutu
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd premise - arguing over Tutu roleplay for a party payment dispute. 'We can't have two Tutus' is instantly quotable.

jokes.text: Larry's confession: 'I've ruined every party I've ever gone to in my entire life. I have bad energy.'
jokes.setup: TRC-style confession session
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-awareness moment, captures the essence of his character in one line

jokes.text: I faked cordiality and I feigned interest. I'm gonna be honest, I'm disappointed. I was expecting more from my childhood hero. I've been expecting more from myself my whole life and it's just not there.
jokes.setup: Larry's honest confession in fake Truth and Reconciliation
jokes.punchline: His admission of lifelong self-disappointment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - brutal self-honesty disguised as therapeutic confession. His admission of chronic self-disappointment is both funny and poignant.

jokes.text: I've ruined every party I've ever gone to in my entire life. I have bad energy. Do you accept that it was a bad vibe, actually, and kind of ruined my party? I really did the best under the circumstances of a person who hates people and yet had to be amongst them.
jokes.setup: Larry's continued brutal self-assessment
jokes.punchline: His explanation of hating people while being forced to socialize
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - Larry's self-awareness about his social toxicity and the contradiction of his situation is perfectly articulated.

jokes.text: Larry writing 'Kramen' then correcting it to 'Simon' for Michael's son
jokes.setup: Larry signing autograph for Michael's son
jokes.punchline: Larry clearly not paying attention to the child's actual name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's inattention to others, mild cringe humor

jokes.text: Asshole! You leave me tip in toilet.
jokes.setup: Housekeeper bursting into Larry's room
jokes.punchline: Revealing Larry actually did put money in the toilet
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - Larry's 'hypothetical' toilet glasses scenario was actually real, and he left a tip in there. Great escalation.

jokes.text: What are you talking about? What? I didn't... I didn't put money in the toilet. You throw my tip in toilet, I toss your clothes. You sick maniac. You're insane. I go out with a bang.
jokes.setup: Larry denying putting tip in toilet
jokes.punchline: Housekeeper's revenge and declaration she's going out 'with a bang'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - the housekeeper's revenge and 'going out with a bang' on her last month is perfectly vindictive and satisfying.

jokes.text: This guy's not African, he's South African.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing to complain about Michael
jokes.punchline: His stubborn insistence on the geographical distinction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's earlier African vs South African distinction. His obsession with this meaningless difference is perfectly petty.

jokes.text: Larry getting arrested for giving water to voters in line, violating Georgia's Election Integrity Act
jokes.setup: Larry offering water to help Auntie Rae in voting line
jokes.punchline: Simple act of kindness being criminalized
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy ending, perfect Curb escalation from simple kindness to arrest, excellent social commentary

jokes.text: Sir! In the navy blazer. Put your hands in the air. You under arrest for violation of the Election Integrity Act. What? What are you talking about? It is illegal for anyone in the state of Georgia to provide food or water to voters in line at the polls.
jokes.setup: Larry giving water to voters in line
jokes.punchline: Being arrested for voter assistance under actual Georgia law
jokes.explanation: Brilliant finale - Larry's one genuinely kind act results in arrest due to actual absurd Georgia voting law. Perfect Curb irony and social commentary.

jokes.text: I'm not even from here! I just came to make an appearance at a party. And I didn't even get paid! I was just being cordial. I was being cordial.
jokes.setup: Larry being arrested
jokes.punchline: His desperate final plea about being cordial
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode ending - Larry's final ironic plea about being cordial while being arrested for an act of genuine kindness. Brings the cordiality theme full circle.
